commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fill triangular gaps in Case 3 split-face extrude

When extruding a split face (Case 3: mixed moved/duplicated vertices),
non_coplanar edges were skipped for wall creation. But when one vertex
of such an edge is moved and the other duplicated, a triangular gap
appears. Now all edges with shifted endpoints get walls, with
deduplication to collapse quads with repeated vertices into triangles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: sistema de nurturing con recordatorios automaticos y panel admin

Implementa flujo completo de nurturing: 3 modelos (NurturingConfig,
NurturingPlantilla, NurturingLog), controlador admin con dashboard,

contactos con acciones individuales/masivas, cron independiente de
recordatorios, desuscripcion publica con token, e integracion con
el panel de mensajes existente.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fix HEMesh corruption from recycled half-edge IDs

When addFace consumed a boundary half-edge, it tried to unlink it from
the boundary chain by following stale next/prev pointers. After ID
recycling via the free list, those pointers could reference completely
different (reused) half-edges. Setting their next/prev to NONE corrupted
face loops, causing faceLoop to crash with "Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'next')" on subsequent extrude operations.

Fix: verify the neighbor actually references BACK to the boundary HE
before modifying it. If the ID was recycled, the bidirectional check
fails and we skip the (corrupting) unlink.

Adds regression test for stair-building sequence:
box → split → extrude → split → extrude.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix GPU contention: RGBA textures + CUDA sync after ESRGAN

- Switch RGB→RGBA: 4bpp rows are naturally 4-byte aligned, allowing
  the GL driver to use faster upload paths (no GL_UNPACK_ALIGNMENT=1)
- Add torch.cuda.synchronize() after enhance() so all ESRGAN GPU work
  completes before the main render loop can observe contention.
  CUDA stream priority does not affect OpenGL (separate APIs).

Add flat prop to H1 heading - disable 3D tilt for blog titles

Blog post H1s appeared tilted and off-center due to the perspective
rotateX/rotateY transform designed for short display titles. The new
flat prop removes the perspective rotation while keeping backing
layer depth via translate offsets.

chore(assistant): raise maxSteps from 5 to 15

The agent stops as soon as it has a final answer — maxSteps is just
a safety limit against infinite loops. 15 steps gives the agent room
for complex multi-tool workflows (inventory check → category drill-down
→ catalog search → alternatives → budget review → final answer).

fix(deploy): use Agave 3.1.9 + fix all seed arrays for edition2024 compat

Agave 2.1.7's BPF platform-tools bundles Cargo 1.79.0 which cannot parse
edition2024 crate manifests (blake3 1.8.3, constant_time_eq 0.4.2). Must
use Agave 3.1.9 which has Cargo 1.85+ in its BPF toolchain.

Fix edition2024 array type inference by adding explicit `as &[u8]` casts
to the first element of all multi-element PDA seed arrays across:
- contract.rs: 7 seed arrays (escrow, contract, poe, agent-identity)
- dispute.rs: 9 seed arrays (dispute, agent-identity, escrow)
- identity.rs: 3 seed arrays (agent-identity)
- insurance.rs: 2 seed arrays (insurance-claim, agent-identity)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip quotes from JSESSIONID cookie value

Go's net/http rejects quote characters in cookie values. The quotes are
part of how LinkedIn stores the JSESSIONID but must be stripped before
setting the cookie. The CSRF token header already did this correctly;
now the cookie jar does too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve global button text clipping by using minHeight instead of height

The Mantine theme set a fixed height: 42px on all buttons which clipped text
when combined with uppercase transform and letter-spacing. Changed to minHeight
so buttons can grow to fit content but won't shrink below 42px.

Also removed redundant height overrides on template card buttons.

BREAKING: Remove Scheduler (cron/queue) view from TUI. Background job
scheduling is retained in src/jobs/scheduler.rs - only the TUI view is
removed.

Changes:
- Delete src/tui/views/scheduler.rs (471 lines)
- Update TuiView enum: remove Scheduler variant, renumber (4 views)
- Update keybindings: key 4 now opens Settings (was Scheduler)
- Update focus system: remove Scheduler panel navigation
- Update app/mod.rs: remove scheduler_view field
- Update render.rs, status_bar.rs: remove Scheduler match arms
- Update home.rs, studio.rs: adjust view switching keys
- Update main.rs: remove scheduler CLI option and help text
- Update header.rs: TAB_NAMES now has 4 entries
- Update wiring_checkpoint_10.rs: tests for 4-view architecture
- Update .gitignore: add generated/ directory

4-View Architecture (v0.22):
1. Studio   - YAML editor + workspace
2. Runner   - Workflow execution monitor
3. Chat     - Conversational agent
4. Settings - Configuration + preferences

commit.message:
Add weighted bucket + evolution bias Pokemon picker

Replace sequential Pokemon unlocking with randomized picking:
- Available uncaught Pokemon split into 5 equal buckets by order
- Bucket probabilities: 50%, 30%, 10%, 9%, 1% (lower IDs favored)
- Fewer than 5 available: pure random pick
- After bucket pick, bias toward base evolution forms (power-of-2 weights)
- Uses pokemon-evolutions.json for evolution chain data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify Sec10_4_1_InCaseItems → §12.5 [skip ci]

The test exercises a plain case statement with case items (expression-list entries and a default entry), each with a blocking assignment body. The case_item grammar — covering both `case_item_expression : statement_or_null` and `default [ : ] statement_or_null` — is defined in the opening syntax (Syntax 12-3) of §12.5 "Case statement". No deeper subclause in §12.5.1–12.5.4 covers plain case items.

fix: make open-window KPI cards timezone-aware

Pass browser timezone (Intl.DateTimeFormat) from frontend to
/v1/stats/breakdown?tz=. StatsService uses
(NOW() AT TIME ZONE '<tz>')::date instead of CURRENT_DATE so
"today", "last 7d" etc. reflect the user's local date, not UTC.

commit.message:
Fix release: native builds per OS with matrix strategy

CGO cross-compilation fails on single runners (no arm64 assembler
on x86_64, no Darwin cross-compiler on Linux). Use a build matrix
with native runners per OS/arch, then combine artifacts into one
release. Generate Homebrew formula from checksums.

commit.message:
Implement reusable vertical-module registration pattern

Replace fragile import-side-effect registration with declarative
AppConfig-based auto-discovery for projections, event types, and
account-role mappings. ProjectionsConfig.ready() now discovers and
validates all vertical module declarations at startup.

- Add declarative manifests to properties, edim, inventory AppConfigs
- Auto-discover projections and event types from AppConfig attributes
- Add startup integrity assertions and duplicate detection
- Add FinancialEventData canonical base class for Pattern B events
- Add generic ModuleAccountMapping model (accounting/mappings.py)
- Remove module-level registration side-effects from all verticals
- Remove legacy _register_*_events() functions from events/types.py
- Add 23-test integrity suite + property regression tests
- Add architecture note with vertical module checklist

Fix SPA routing: remove old GitHub Pages 404 redirect hack

The 404.html had a GitHub Pages SPA hack that converted /map to /?/map,
breaking client-side routing on Cloudflare Pages. Replaced with a simple
fallback that lets React Router handle all routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove log/checkpoint/self-improver features from beeops scope

These features (session logging, mid-session checkpoints, self-improvement
analysis) are personal development tools that don't belong in the
orchestration package. They caused hook conflicts with user's personal
setup and bloated the package beyond its core purpose of Issue execution.

Removed:
- hooks: run-log.py, checkpoint.py, resolve-log-path.py
- skills: bo-log-writer, bo-self-improver
- contexts: log.md, fb.md (all locales)
- agent-modes: BO_FB_AGENT, BO_FB_INCLUDE_FB entries, skip_log field
- worker-base: external skill references (dev-error-resolver, dev-tdd-workflow,
  meta-resource-router, code-reviewer agent)

beeops now focuses on orchestration: 10 skills, 1 hook (UserPromptSubmit).
